[{"text":["The first contact with the Vosges was focused on the ascent and descent of Ballon d’Alsace, en route to Belfort, where attackers prevailed. Today is a proper rollercoaster with four major climbs and an elevation gain of 3,800 metres packed in155.3 km of racing between Mulhouse and Le Markstein Fellering.
The peloton are set to roll at 13:10, to reach km 0 at 13:30. The winners will be crowned at around 17:30."],"locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:41:15+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:41:42+02:00","slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-14-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","type":"liv","id":1347080,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:40:15+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 14 of the Tour de France 2026","highlight":true,"picto":"liv_start","stage":14,"_id":"445dbb014a6f9230279736b93534bdc47a22e30a0cd33aa22b15c7b7e8c20083","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371302526,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-14-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","model":"news"},{"id":23415,"isClub":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=xaq6q4u","name":"Sprint intermédiaire - Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. This is the fourth time this mountain pass is tackled via a significant ascent in the Tour de France. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"1 minute","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_1min_away","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"slug":"1-minute-14","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","locale":"en","text":["UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers."],"createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T14:11:35+02:00","id":1347403,"type":"liv","_id":"6d6f4e240abc837366c70c8f70b7c76e53aa8c176b446d668f21da8494d3d2e0","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376695335,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-minute-14","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","text":["Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. Caja Rural-Seguros RGA: Sebastian Berwick"],"locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","slug":"a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","_id":"427cf9a146ecc5ff4e2aaf209d14f038f4e6f61b75d4fb56f0f366337b34553d","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376886723,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","model":"news"},{"text":["A two-time winner of the Tour de France in 1938 and 1948, the legendary Gino Bartali (1914–2000) was born on 18 July in Ponte a Ema, near Florence. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.

Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de \nCaja Rural-Seguros RGA: Sebastian Berwick"],"locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","slug":"a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","_id":"427cf9a146ecc5ff4e2aaf209d14f038f4e6f61b75d4fb56f0f366337b34553d","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376886723,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","model":"news"},{"text":["A two-time winner of the Tour de France in 1938 and 1948, the legendary Gino Bartali (1914–2000) was born on 18 July in Ponte a Ema, near Florence. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de \nCaja Rural-Seguros RGA: Sebastian Berwick"],"locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","slug":"a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","_id":"427cf9a146ecc5ff4e2aaf209d14f038f4e6f61b75d4fb56f0f366337b34553d","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376886723,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","model":"news"},{"text":["A two-time winner of the Tour de France in 1938 and 1948, the legendary Gino Bartali (1914–2000) was born on 18 July in Ponte a Ema, near Florence. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de
