In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347192,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:16:42+02:00","slug":"white-jersey-49","text":["Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) retained the white jersey in Chalon-sur-Saône, with a gap of 13’’ to Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM) and 46’’ to Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates-XRG)."],"locale":"en","picto":"liv_white_jersey","highlight":false,"stage":14,"title":"White jersey","pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"fa5770fb8e27d56564bc76191b297c139f4be75de6a6a02ee1ffe34e6bea5c75","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784373403130,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:white-jersey-49","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_team_ranking","stage":14,"title":"Team classification","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347206,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:20:39+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de \nCaja Rural-Seguros RGA: Sebastian Berwick"],"locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T14:14:46+02:00","slug":"a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","_id":"427cf9a146ecc5ff4e2aaf209d14f038f4e6f61b75d4fb56f0f366337b34553d","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784376886723,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-first-composition-pidcock-jorgenson-carapaz","model":"news"},{"text":["A two-time winner of the Tour de France in 1938 and 1948, the legendary Gino Bartali (1914–2000) was born on 18 July in Ponte a Ema, near Florence. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
\nSimon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. He won stage 5 of the Tour de France 2022in Wallers-Arenberg and retired at the beginning of 2026, after the Tour Down Under and the Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race."],"locale":"en","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","slug":"thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:04:07+02:00","id":1347124,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Thinking of Bartali and Clarke","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"_id":"3f590dd0820f79c0fd42d918d577cdba8ce56ed05dcd90c6b50b2f0d37a02ebd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784372647864,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:thinking-of-bartali-and-clarke","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
\nToday marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. With three riders in the chasing group — Bruno Armirail, Victor Campenaerts and Matteo Jorgenson — the Visma-Lease a Bike team opens up a range of strategic possibilities. These riders could simply aim for the stage win, particularly Jorgenson, or act as a bridge for their leader Jonas Vingegaard, in case of an attack on an upcoming climb. Alternatively, they could provide support later in the stage if they are reeled back in by the peloton. However, as the gap to the peloton shrinks rapidly, the window for executing such strategies is narrowing. That said, the Dutch team holds the upper hand over UAE Team Emirates-XRG for now. Meanwhile, Tadej Pogacar’s squad is forced to set a high pace at the front of the peloton due to the presence of Tom Pidcock (4th in the general classification) in the chasing group. The Emirati team will likely need to maintain this tempo for many kilometres, as keeping the gap tight could derail the strategy Visma-Lease a Bike has devised.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:12:00+00:00"},"text":"Interesting. 3 of the riders of Visma are in the breakaway and UAE riders are in the peloton. What could this means in terms of strategy or how the race pans out in the next few hours ? This climb begins at Saint-Amarin (km 138.2) and follows a road with an uneven surface and challenging gradients, levelling off as it passes through the village of Geishouse. The most spectacular moment comes just afterwards, 4.5 kilometres from the summit, when the route enters a greenway (normally accessible only to cyclists and pedestrians) that winds through the forest towards the farm known as ‘Haag’, from which the pass takes its name. The most demanding section comes one and a half kilometres from the summit with gradients of up to 16 per cent. A good indication of the difficulty involved is that several teams taking part in this Tour, such as UAE, Decathlon and Lidl-Trek, have come specifically for recons."],"title":"The debut of the Col du Haag","stage":14,"highlight":false,"picto":"liv_elevation","stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"9c7a1f58362bc4a20516723fe74a88d8715429dd30507d174922e5c27e28873c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784386801895,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:the-debut-of-the-col-du-haag","model":"news"},{"text":["+30'' Vingegaard, Siexas, Del Toro

","response":{"text":"Hello ! The Visma – Lease a Bike riders in the chase group — Bruno Armirail, Victor Campenaerts and Matteo Jorgenson — are setting a high pace, even with fourth-placed overall rider Tom Pidcock (Pinarello – Q36.5) alongside them. Their goal is to maintain or even widen the gap to the peloton. By getting a head start, they could serve as points of support for their leader Jonas Vingegaard (2nd overall) later in the stage in case of an attack. This allows them to preempt the likely acceleration by Yellow Jersey Tadej Pogacar’s UAE Emirates-XRG team in the finale.","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-18T13:57:34+00:00","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:57:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com"}},"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T13:57:00+00:00","_id":"question-6a5b863daf4acd77d8021d8d","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784383054963,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":false,"picto":"liv_story","stage":14,"title":"Le Markstein, a special memory for Pogacar","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1347979,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T16:37:08+02:00","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["“I’m really looking forward to riding at Le Markstein and to enjoy the crowd”, Tadej Pogacar said as he prepared to return to a venue he had already conquered. It was back in 2023, on stage 20, the first to finish there.
At the beginning of the third week, the Slovenian, a 2-time winner of the Tour at the time, had suffered the rule of Jonas Vingegaard in the Combloux ITT (stage 16) and the following day, when he crumbled on the slopes of Col de la Loze (stage 17). Three days later, the final mountain stage of the Tour was initially animated by Thibaut Pinot, who bid a spectacular farewell to the thousands of fans gathered to celebrate him, before being reeled in by the top GC contenders inside the last 5 kilometres of ascent, on the slopes of Col du Platzerwasel.

His record at the Tour de France includes two King of the Mountains titles, again in 1938 and 1948, as well as 12 stage victories spread across his eight appearances between 1937 and 1953.
Simon Clarke was also born on 18 July, but in 1986. Do sprinters and climbers have different specialized bikes from their teammates ? ","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! There are indeed different types of bikes. In addition to time-trial bikes, used exclusively for that discipline, Tour de France riders use specific bikes for road stages like today’s, either mountain-oriented models (lightweight for easier climbing, with geometry designed for agility and handling) or machines built for flat terrain and high speeds. Those are more aerodynamic than the former and offer greater responsiveness and stiffness. Consequently, the choice of bike depends largely on the stage profile, whether the peloton faces mountain passes or rides on flat terrain. However, this distinction is becoming less common given the general increase in the peloton's average speed and the ever-improving performance of modern bikes, it is not unusual to see pure climbers riding aerodynamic bikes in the mountains.","createdAt":"2026-07-18T11:43:47+00:00"},"event":{"bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"updatedBy":null},"widget":"involve","locale":"en","_id":"question-6a5b5f9698816511170d8904","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784375027856,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"pinned":false,"stageType":"HMG","highlight":true,"picto":"liv_lead_rid","stage":14,"title":"Carapaz attacks","updatedBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T17:07:37+02:00","id":1348109,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","text":["The Ecuadorian goes solo with just under 9 kilometres remaining on the ascent.

Tour","stage":14,"picto":"liv_story","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"bvittek","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","id":1347094,"type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.
Today marks the 14th stage start from Mulhouse. The most recent, in 2019, gave way to a summit finish at La Planche des Belles Filles. Dylan Teuns won the stage while Giulio Ciccone took the Maillot Jaune from Julian Alaphilippe."],"slug":"mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","createdAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-18T12:47:30+02:00","_id":"d0387e97bde6de7bf9dc866b2a892d9ec883c713110a7403b2ad0496272c6c0c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784371650774,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:mulhouse-a-classic-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","pinned":false,"title":"Paret-Peintre conquers another summit","stage":14,"picto":"liv_top_2","highlight":true,"updatedBy":"bvittek","id":1347683,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T15:28:05+02:00","type":"liv","createdBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"locale":"en","text":["The French climber is up to 33 KOM points after going first at Col du Page.

To cap off the return of a women’s Tour de France, the final week-end was set in the Vosges with two gruelling stages, especially on the penultimate day of the race. From Sélestat to Le Markstein, the 127.1-km route featured three major ascents: Petit Ballon, Platzerwasel, and Grand Ballon, summited inside the final 10 kilometres.
Annemiek van Vleuten attacked on the Petit Ballon, with only Demi Vollering following her. AVV went solo on the Platzerwasel and covered the final 62 kilometres alone to open massive gaps and take the Maillot Jaune at the finish, on the eve of her overall triumph at La Super Planche des Belles Filles. Zigart finished 8th at Le Markstein, 7’23’’ behind the Dutch winner."],"locale":"en","year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","type":"liv","id":1348018,"publicationAt":"2026-07-18T16:45:02+02:00","_id":"5c5d21c2f209edf705b5e2273a383c0985c252c001c94773504c726af3633eca","_bind":"publication_en-2026-14","_origin":"news-2026-14","_updatedAt":1784385902893,"_parent":"stage-2026:8087e0289dce432fecf8e6710b9f0ee5e33378d4480ca3001b0237c990d4ab31","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:le-markstein-also-an-iconic-venue-of-the-tour-de-france-femmes-avec-zwift","model":"news"},{"pinned":false,"createdBy":"ganz","highlight":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","id":8388,"onlyDataTab":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-18T17:35:39+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-18T17:35:39+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-18T17:32:00+02:00","onlyClubTab":false,"type":"twitter","url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2078503230359613688","stage":{"isCancelled":false,"length":155.3,"timezone":"Europe/Paris","lengthDisplay":155.5,"stage":14,"type":"HMG","podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","date":"2026-07-18T00:00:00+02:00","showGroups":true},"socialContentLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Isaac As in all professional road cycling races, the Tour de France rules state that "regardless of a rider's position in the race, mechanical assistance (whether for a wheel or bike change or any other mechanical issue) is permitted only at the rear of the peloton they are in, on the right-hand side of the road, and while stationary." This is done for safety reasons, to prevent team cars from constantly overtaking the peloton. Furthermore, mechanical assistance from a fixed position located further up the race is strictly prohibited."},"_id":"6a5b820897aaba2d40044896","text":"Why is assistance always provided from behind ? Wouldn't it be more efficient to overtake the rider and wait for them with a new bike that is already ready ?
